title: Erica notholeeana
intro: Erica notholeeana, also known as the Notholeean Heath, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). The Notholeean Heath has been traditionally used to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The Notholeean Heath is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to support respiratory health and soothe digestive issues.
taxonomy: Erica notholeeana is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a member of the genus Erica, commonly known as heaths. The plant is native to Western Australia and is often found growing in sandy soils. Erica notholeeana is a low-growing shrub that produces white or yellow flowers. This species is classified as a shrub in the Ericaceae family, which also includes other heath and heather species.
